The Calm Before the Storm... and the Calm AfterThere are two kinds of silence, and both are lies.
The first lies because it promises that the world is still capable of being saved. The second lies because it convinces you that everything is over. Between these two deceptions lives the human being—a creature condemned to consciousness, as though consciousness were not a gift, but existence's final punishment upon itself.
The calm before the storm is not tranquility; it is the universe's temporary contempt. Existence, in a rare moment of boredom, postpones its blow—not out of mercy, but because it knows that waiting perfects the strike. In that moment, the sky appears innocent, and everything becomes absurdly familiar. The chair remains where it has always been, the clock continues counting time, and faces continue to smile as though they had learned nothing from the history of suffering.
Yet the truth is that everything has already been decided. The storm does not begin when the winds rise; it begins when existence decides that its ordinary laws are no longer sufficient to remind you of your insignificance.
Then the storm arrives—not to destroy, but to correct an illusion. It is not an extraordinary event; it is the world's original condition. Order, peace, and hope are merely brief intermissions that absurdity takes before resuming its work.
And when it ends...
Another calm is born.
But it is not the calm of survivors, for no one truly survives. It is the calm of one who has discovered that survival was only a linguistic invention created by the fearful to give themselves a reason to wake each morning. What follows the storm is not a new life, but the remains of an old one that has lost its ability to deceive its owner.
There, silence becomes even more merciless because it no longer conceals anything. The stage has been exposed, the scenery has collapsed, the actors have departed, and all that remains is an empty platform echoing with the one question that has found no answer since humanity's birth:
Why is there something rather than nothing?
Perhaps the question itself was the first mistake.
Existence does not explain itself because it owes no one an explanation. It simply is—a blind presence that knows neither justice, nor purpose, nor meaning. Humanity, in its naïve arrogance, stands before this cosmic silence demanding an answer, as though the void were somehow obligated to justify its own emptiness.
The calm before the storm was the fear of the unknown.
The calm after the storm is the realization that the unknown was never your enemy; it was the illusion that protected you from the truth.
And the truth is terrifying in its simplicity:
The universe is not against you...
because it has never noticed that you exist.
Perhaps that is why the final silence feels colder than death itself. It is not the end of life, but the end of the belief that life hides a secret waiting to be discovered. Once that belief collapses, all that remains is for a person to carry consciousness like a stone that cannot be set down, and continue walking a road that leads not to salvation, but to ever greater clarity.
And the cruelest form of clarity is this:
The storm was never the exception in your life.
The calm was.
BY ✍🏻 Rose AlMuhessen
rosekma214@gmail.com
